message:
qdrant: gate testcontainer suite + fix upsert/query visibility race

## Why

Two follow-ups to the qdrant testcontainer lifecycle suite that
landed in 658cd606:

### 1. Feature-gate the testcontainer deps

`testcontainers` + `tokio` pull in a heavy native stack (bollard,
hyperlocal, hyper-named-pipe, the Docker credential plumbing, ~40
transitive crates), and spinning the suite up requires a running
Docker daemon. A developer doing an offline build, or CI on a
machine without a Docker socket, has no business compiling any of
that.

The fix is to declare both deps as `optional = true` in
`[dependencies]` (they have to be in `[dependencies]`, not
`[dev-dependencies]`, because Cargo disallows `optional = true` on
dev-deps) and introduce a new `integration-testcontainer` feature
that activates them via `dep:testcontainers` / `dep:tokio`. Default
builds are now offline-friendly and docker-less; a developer
opting into the real-Qdrant suite runs

    cargo test -p fcp-qdrant --features integration-testcontainer

### 2. Remove the upsert-to-query visibility race

Qdrant's `upsert_points` endpoint returns HTTP 200 the moment the
write is accepted by the WAL, but the affected points don't become
visible to `count`, `scroll`, or `search` until the indexer flushes
the segment. The previous test body called `count` immediately after
`upsert_points` and asserted the exact expected inventory, which
worked on a warm cluster but raced on a cold testcontainer where
the first segment flush hasn't happened yet.

Introduces `wait_for_exact_count(client, name, expected)` that
polls `count(exact: true)` every 100ms for up to 5s. Replaces the
three immediate-`count` assertions in
`upsert_points_persist_and_count_reflects_inventory` (post-upsert,
post-delete) and `search_ranks_points_by_true_cosine_similarity`
(post-upsert, before ranking) with polling calls. The poll budget
is intentionally generous so CI flakes don't get blamed on the
test harness.

## Cargo.lock

The Cargo.lock delta (testcontainers + tokio added to the
fcp-qdrant dep list, lines 5013+) is the Cargo-resolver reflection
of declaring the optional dev-adjacent deps in
connectors/qdrant/Cargo.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -37,10 +37,24 @@ tracing.workspace = true
 tracing-subscriber.workspace = true
 uuid.workspace = true
 
+# Integration-testcontainer deps — only pulled in when the
+# `integration-testcontainer` feature is enabled. Kept in
+# `[dependencies]` (not `[dev-dependencies]`) because Cargo disallows
+# `optional = true` on dev-dependencies; they are feature-gated so
+# default builds stay offline-friendly and docker-less.
+testcontainers = { version = "0.23", optional = true }
+tokio = { workspace = true, optional = true }
+
 [dev-dependencies]
 ciborium.workspace = true
 wiremock.workspace = true
 futures-util.workspace = true
 fcp-crypto = { path = "../../crates/fcp-crypto" }
 fcp-manifest = { path = "../../crates/fcp-manifest" }
 fcp-testkit = { path = "../../crates/fcp-testkit" }
+
+[features]
+default = []
+# Enable real-Qdrant testcontainer integration tests. Requires a
+# running Docker daemon. See tests/lifecycle_integration.rs.
+integration-testcontainer = ["dep:testcontainers", "dep:tokio"]
